session_hint: the Node.js email queue processor memory growth investigation
question: What was causing the Node.js email queue processor memory to grow without bound?
turns: Our Node.js 18 email queue worker's RSS climbs from 120MB to 2GB over about 6 hours and then crashes. First theory: closures inside the processJob function are retaining large email body strings after each job completes.

Took V8 heap snapshots at 30-minute intervals using --inspect and Chrome DevTools Memory tab. String objects in the heap stay constant at ~8MB. GC is collecting email body strings correctly after each job finishes. No closure retention visible. Ruling out closure-retained strings.

Second theory: EventEmitter listeners are accumulating — the job processor calls emitter.on('data', ...) per job without a matching removeListener. Checked listenerCount(emitter, 'data') after each job — it stays at exactly 3 (three permanent handlers added at startup). No listener accumulation. Ruling out EventEmitter listener leak.

Found the actual bug by diffing heap snapshots on the Timers category. Timer count grows by exactly 1 per job processed — now at 14,000 timers after 14,000 jobs. Traced to a metrics helper called inside processJob: `setInterval(() => recordMetrics(jobId), 1000)` — it creates a new interval for every job but the return value is discarded and clearInterval is never called. Each dangling interval holds a reference to the jobId closure. Fixed by storing the timer handle and calling clearInterval after job completion. Memory stabilized at 130MB. Root cause: setInterval created per job with no corresponding clearInterval, accumulating dangling timers.
